hints.Medio: 'Medio' is an adjective/noun in the ablative meaning 'in the middle'
hints.None: There is a finite verb — 'ibis' is 2nd person singular future of 'ire'

hints.Present indicative: Present 2nd person of 'ire' would be 'is' — 'ibis' has the future -bi- marker
hints.Imperfect indicative: Imperfect would be 'ibas' — 'ibis' with -bis ending is future

hints.sum, esse, fui, futurus: These are the principal parts of 'esse' (to be)
hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the principal parts of 'ferre' (to carry)
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are the principal parts of 'dare' (to give)

hints.Nominative: Nominative neuter of 'medius' would be 'medium' — 'medio' with -o ending is dative or ablative
hints.Dative: Dative is possible for 'medio', but here it functions as an ablative of place/route
hints.Accusative: Accusative neuter would be 'medium' — 'medio' is ablative

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'medium' with -um ending is 2nd declension neuter
hints.3rd: 3rd declension has varied endings — 'medium' is a standard 2nd declension neuter noun
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns have genitive -us — 'medium' has genitive 'medii'

hints.Positive: Positive degree would be 'tutus' — 'tutissimus' with -issimus is superlative
hints.Comparative: Comparative would be 'tutior' — 'tutissimus' with -issimus ending is superlative
hints.Elative: Elative uses the superlative form but means 'very safe' — here the true superlative 'safest' fits the context

translation: You will go safest in the middle
